Patch Set 3:

Test failures are all due to timeouts. The conf-sanity test I wrote seems to work. Should I update the commit message w/ "Test-Parameters: testlist=conf-sanity" and let a retest occur?

Patch Set 3:

Since this patch is only affecting conf-sanity, you can cut out almost all of the other testing (which doesn't provide any value for this patch) via:

 Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=conf-sanity

Without the "trivial" keyword, any change to the commit message would cause a new build and trigger another full round of testing, in addition to an extra conf-sanity run, which isn't what you want.

With the "trivial" keyword, it only does a single "review-ldiskfs" test run, which is sanity.sh and lnet-selftest.sh to verify basic functionality, and then whatever additional tests are requested via "testlist=".

parents.subject: LU-6142 lnet: replace white spaces with tabs for LNet core

commit.subject: LU-8100 tests: Test the correctness of target_obd
commit.message:
LU-8100 tests: Test the correctness of target_obd

The target_obd proc file is what is used by lfs to list mdts
of a lustre file system. Added a conf-sanity test to ensure
correctness of lfs mdts output.

Test-Parameters: trivial mdtfilesystemtype=ldiskfs mdsfilesystemtype=ldiskfs ostfilesystemtype=ldiskfs mdtcount=1 testlist=conf-sanity
Test-Parameters: trivial mdtfilesystemtype=ldiskfs mdsfilesystemtype=ldiskfs ostfilesystemtype=ldiskfs mdtcount=2 mdtcount=4 testlist=conf-sanity
Test-Parameters: trivial mdtfilesystemtype=zfs mdsfilesystemtype=zfs ostfilesystemtype=zfs mdtcount=1 testlist=conf-sanity

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Di Natale <dinatale2@llnl.gov>
Change-Id: Ia876ed456b95570b90bc90f05d8b7b97e1aa71af
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/20546
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
